items.q: Why is HDI a better measure of development than GNI per capita?
items.correct: HDI combines income, life expectancy and education, capturing quality of life — GNI per capita is just average income and hides inequality and non-economic wellbeing.
items.misconception: Because HDI measures how happy people are.
items.random_wrong: Because HDI is easier to calculate than GNI.
items.why: Students treat HDI as a happiness index. It is broader than GNI but still only captures three measurable dimensions — not subjective wellbeing or environmental quality.

items.correct: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs — balancing economic, social and environmental goals.
items.misconception: Sustainable development means stopping all economic growth.
items.random_wrong: Sustainable development means using only solar power.
items.why: Students treat sustainability as anti-development. The Brundtland definition is about balance and intergenerational equity — not zero growth or one specific technology.

items.q: Why does deforestation increase flood risk?
items.correct: Trees intercept rainfall, their roots increase infiltration and transpiration removes water — removing them increases surface runoff and reduces lag time, peaking river levels faster.
items.misconception: Deforestation causes floods because trees normally hold water in their branches forever.
items.random_wrong: Deforestation has no effect on rivers — only rainfall matters.
items.why: Interception is temporary but it delays and reduces runoff. Without forests, water reaches rivers faster and in greater volume — interacting with antecedent soil moisture to drive flood peaks.
It's Raworth. “Stiglitz” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which country uses Gross National Happiness?
items.why: It's Bhutan. “Norway” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Exact answer: 17 goals. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
